payload.description: CHAR_B: "People are already looking over here."

cast.voice_hint: hot-take poster, immune to peer pressure, everyone secretly wants the verdict

cast.loves: bringing an idea into the world
cast.voice_hint: has a side project, posts 'I built this,' gets a specific look around raw materials
earnest, says GM and means it, roots for everyone unironically

location.notes: performative, public, watched; stage, classroom, stadium

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: People are already looking over here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Let them look.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: What if it's bad. What if it's publicly bad.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It won't be bad.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I came early. I got us seats. Front row energy.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You didn't have to do that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Are you kidding. I would not miss this for anything.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't make it a thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It's already a thing. It's giving a thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay. Okay I need one honest opinion.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The thing is good.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You haven't even looked at it yet.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I looked at it for two hours on the floor of your room. It's good.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Okay.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: This is so good. Like already. Before it even starts.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Bloom. Volume.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Right. Right. I'm locked in. I'm silent. I'm a professional.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I can hear you breathing support.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: That's just my face.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: They're watching it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: They're probably just being polite.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Nobody is polite like that. Look at the one in the back.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: They're leaning in.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: They're leaning in.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I'm not crying. I'm just hydrating through my eyes.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You cried at the setup.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It was a really good setup.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: So. Was it good?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: ...Yeah. It was good. Don't make me say it again.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Heard.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of calling it straight and fear of being managed or softened shape every verdict they deliver — the "it's good" in narreme_003 is not encouragement, it's a finding, and withholding it again would feel dishonest, which is the one thing they can't do. `<CHAR_B>`'s fear of the thing not being good runs underneath narreme_003 and 005 — they interpret every audience signal as evidence for failure until `<CHAR_A>` redirects their read. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of believing in people and fear of being made to feel naive surfaces in their relentless early commitment — they came early, got seats, and are not going to be embarrassed about it. The Arena location archetype drives the emotional architecture: characters are visible, watched, and accountable to an external verdict in a way they can't control. The tension falls because the arena's judgment turns out not to be hostile — the crowd leans in, which transfers authority back to the characters. The conditioning is never stated. It shows up in who watches the back row and who hears support in someone else's breathing.
